meta.description: Persuasive Essays for Rating, Selecting, and Understanding Argumentative and Discourse Elements — a large-scale corpus of 25,000+ argumentative essays by US middle and high school students with holistic and trait-level scores.

summary.note: TLA's most widely cited dataset. De facto benchmark in the AES field, consistently appearing alongside or replacing older benchmarks like ASAP. Notable uses include LLM scoring fairness, socioeconomic writing score gaps, cognitive writing model validation, and adversarial robustness.

papers.title: Composable Cross-prompt Essay Scoring by Merging Models
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely direct — cross-prompt AES experiments typically use PERSUADE as a benchmark corpus
papers.key_takeaway: Proposes source-free model merging for cross-prompt AES; PERSUADE likely used as primary evaluation corpus.

papers.title: A Comprehensive Analysis of Large Language Model Outputs: Similarity, Diversity, and Bias
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — PERSUADE possibly used as a human-written text baseline for comparison against LLM outputs
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. PERSUADE likely used as a human writing benchmark to contrast with LLM-generated text.

papers.title: DAMAGE: Detecting Adversarially Modified AI Generated Text
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — PERSUADE likely used as a human-written baseline for AI detection experiments
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. PERSUADE likely serves as human-written ground truth for training or evaluating AI text detectors.

papers.title: Leveraging Small LLMs for Argument Mining in Education
papers.use_of_dataset: Indirect — uses Feedback Prize dataset which overlaps with PERSUADE; likely cited as a related resource
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. Argument component segmentation and classification on student essays; PERSUADE adjacent via shared Kaggle competition data.

papers.title: Machine-assisted Writing Evaluation: Exploring Pre-trained Language Models in Analyzing Argumentative Moves
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — PERSUADE cited as a related argumentative writing corpus
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. Uses a separate longitudinal Chinese learner corpus but PERSUADE likely referenced as a key benchmark in the argumentative writing space.

papers.title: Measuring Non-Adversarial Reproduction of Training Data in Large Language Models
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — PERSUADE 2.0 explicitly used as a source of argumentative writing prompts in the experimental setup
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE prompts used to study how much LLMs reproduce training data during natural interactions — a high-profile study involving Google DeepMind and ETH Zurich testing GPT, Claude, Llama, and Gemini models.

papers.title: Assessing Academic Language in Tenth Grade Essays Using Natural Language Processing
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — 20,820 source-based argumentative essays used as the primary corpus
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE used as the primary large-scale corpus to examine academic language features and their relationship to writing quality.

papers.title: Examining the Responsible Use of Zero-shot AI Approaches to Scoring Essays
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely direct — PERSUADE commonly used in ETS-adjacent AES research as an evaluation corpus
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. Evaluates GPT-4o zero-shot essay scoring with emphasis on fairness and explainability; PERSUADE likely used as evaluation corpus.

papers.title: Can AI Provide Useful Holistic Essay Scoring?
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — cited as one of three extant corpora used for ChatGPT scoring evaluation
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE used as one of three corpora to evaluate ChatGPT holistic essay scoring against human raters.

papers.title: Charting L2 Argumentative Writing: A Systematic Review
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference — systematic review of 108 articles on L2 argumentative writing; PERSUADE possibly cited as a key corpus in the field
papers.key_takeaway: Weak-to-moderate connection. PERSUADE likely referenced as a notable large-scale argumentative writing resource within the broader review.

papers.title: Operationalizing Automated Essay Scoring: A Human-Aware Approach
institutions: Brightlands Institute for a Smart Society
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely direct — PERSUADE commonly used as benchmark corpus in AES comparison studies
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. Compares ML-based AES with LLMs across bias, robustness, and explainability dimensions; PERSUADE likely used as evaluation corpus.

papers.title: Assessing LLM Text Detection in Educational Contexts: Does Human Contribution Affect Detection?
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — PERSUADE likely used as a human-written baseline corpus for benchmarking AI text detectors
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. Introduces GEDE dataset; PERSUADE likely cited as a key human-written essay resource.

papers.title: Does the Prompt-based Large Language Model Recognize Students' Demographics and Introduce Bias in Essay Scoring?
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — 25,000+ student argumentative essays used as primary dataset for fairness analysis
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE used as primary corpus to investigate GPT-4o scoring bias related to gender and first-language background.

papers.title: Can Generative AI Figure out Figurative Language? The Influence of Idioms on Essay Scoring
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely direct — 348 student essays drawn from a corpus; PERSUADE likely the source corpus
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. Examines how idiom presence affects ChatGPT, Gemini, and DeepSeek scoring accuracy; PERSUADE likely the source of essay samples.

papers.title: How to Generalize the Detection of AI-Generated Text: Confounding Neurons
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — PERSUADE likely used as a human-written baseline for out-of-distribution detection experiments
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. Proposes neuron-level intervention to reduce domain-specific bias in AI text detectors; PERSUADE likely one of several evaluation corpora.

papers.title: FairAIED: Navigating Fairness, Bias, and Ethics in Educational AI Applications
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference — survey paper covering fairness in educational AI; PERSUADE likely cited as a key evaluation resource
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. PERSUADE likely referenced as a notable dataset in the fairness and bias section of this comprehensive survey.

papers.title: MAGIC: Multi-Agent Argumentation and Grammar Integrated Critiquer
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — PERSUADE likely cited as a related AES benchmark dataset
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. MAGIC evaluated on GRE essays; PERSUADE likely referenced as a standard benchmark in the AES landscape.

papers.title: Developing a CEFR-based Diagnostic Test to Assess Japanese University Students' Productive Knowledge of Lexical Bundles
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely incidental — focus is on lexical bundle assessment; PERSUADE citation appears peripheral
papers.key_takeaway: Weak connection. PERSUADE likely cited as a background resource on writing corpora; no direct experimental use evident.

papers.title: Argumentative Essay Assessment with LLMs: A Critical Scoping Review
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely direct — review of 46 AAES studies; PERSUADE likely mapped as one of the key datasets in the field
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE likely identified as a primary benchmark dataset in the AAES landscape across the reviewed studies.

papers.title: Toward LLM-Supported Automated Assessment of Critical Thinking Subskills
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely direct — student argumentative essays used as the corpus for critical thinking subskill annotation and scoring
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE likely used as the source corpus for developing and evaluating critical thinking subskill assessment; directly relevant to TLA's CT scoring work.

papers.title: Readability Reconsidered: A Cross-Dataset Analysis of Reference-Free Metrics
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely one of five English datasets used to evaluate readability metrics
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. PERSUADE used as one of several benchmark corpora to evaluate readability metrics against human judgments.

papers.title: Corpus-assisted Counterargumentation Instruction: Cultivating Critical Thinking via Argumentative Writing
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference — PERSUADE cited as a key large-scale argumentative writing corpus in the background literature
papers.key_takeaway: Weak-to-moderate connection. Focus is on corpus-assisted instruction for EFL learners; PERSUADE likely cited as a background resource.

papers.title: Extracting Interpretable Writing Traits from a Large Language Model
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely direct — PERSUADE commonly used in ETS-adjacent AWE research as a primary evaluation corpus
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. Introduces BIOT method for aligning LLM embeddings with interpretable writing traits; PERSUADE likely used as the evaluation corpus.

papers.title: Developing the Preliminary Essay Bundles List (EBL) and Its Applicability to EAP
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — PERSUADE 2.0 explicitly cited as one of five corpora used to extract lexical bundles
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. PERSUADE 2.0 used as a source corpus for extracting argumentative essay lexical bundles alongside LOCNESS, MICUSP, ICNALE, and BAWE.

papers.title: Can LLMs Write Faithfully? An Agent-Based Evaluation of LLM-generated Islamic Content
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely incidental — focus is on Islamic content generation; PERSUADE citation appears peripheral
papers.key_takeaway: Weak connection. Citation appears tangential; likely referenced as a writing quality benchmark in background literature.

papers.title: LAILA: A Large Trait-Based Dataset for Arabic Automated Essay Scoring
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — PERSUADE cited as a key existing AES dataset when motivating LAILA's development
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. PERSUADE referenced as a prominent English AES benchmark to contrast with the gap in Arabic AES resources.

papers.title: FEAT-writing: An Interactive Training System for Argumentative Writing
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — PERSUADE likely cited as a key argumentative writing corpus in the argument mining background
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. FEAT-writing provides automated feedback on argumentative units; PERSUADE likely cited as a benchmark resource.

papers.title: Detecting AI-generated Essays Using Fine-tuned XLNet-CNN Hybrid Techniques
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference — uses EnglishQA Essays Corpus as primary dataset; PERSUADE likely cited as a related human-written essay resource
papers.key_takeaway: Weak-to-moderate connection. PERSUADE likely referenced as background; primary dataset is EnglishQA Essays Corpus.

papers.title: Augmenting AI Scoring of Essays with GPT-Generated Responses
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely direct — ETS research commonly uses PERSUADE for AES experiments; likely used as primary or supplementary corpus
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. Examines GPT-generated essay augmentation for scoring model training; PERSUADE likely used as the student essay corpus.

papers.title: Increasing the Generalizability of Similarity-Based Essay Scoring Through Cross-Prompt Training
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — used alongside ASAP as one of two primary evaluation datasets for cross-prompt scoring experiments
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE used as a primary benchmark; cross-prompt QWK improved from .26 to .55 using the proposed method.

papers.title: A Comparative Study of ML Approaches for Detecting AI-Generated Essays
institutions: Alexandru Ioan Cuza University of Iasi
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely direct — PERSUADE commonly used as human-written baseline in AI detection studies; likely one of the validation datasets
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. Proposes two-step Transformer training for AI essay detection; PERSUADE likely used as a human-written evaluation corpus.

papers.title: Argument Mining in Education: Exploring the Potential of Open-source Small LLMs for Argument Classification and Assessment
papers.use_of_dataset: Indirect — uses Feedback Prize dataset which overlaps with PERSUADE; likely cited as a related resource
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection.

papers.title: Construction of a Korean Semantic Dependency Analysis Algorithm for Large-Scale Corpora
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely incidental — focus is entirely on Korean NLP; PERSUADE citation appears to be a Scholar false positive
papers.key_takeaway: Weak connection. Almost certainly a false positive — no evident connection to PERSUADE.

papers.title: Domain-Adaptive Automated Essay Scoring with Topic Relevance Learning
papers.use_of_dataset: Peripheral — mentioned as a suggested dataset for future work only
papers.key_takeaway: Weak connection. Paper uses original 2012 ASAP dataset; PERSUADE briefly noted as a direction for future experiments.

papers.title: Investigating Adversarial Robustness in LLM-based Automated Essay Scoring
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely direct — ETS AES research commonly uses PERSUADE; likely used to train/evaluate the DeBERTa-based scoring model
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. Shows fine-tuned DeBERTa AES model is highly susceptible to text duplication attacks; PERSUADE likely the primary corpus.

papers.title: Extending Human and AI Annotations beyond Argumentative Writing: A Model Transfer Inquiry
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — PERSUADE cited as foundational prior work on argumentative essay annotation
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. Extends annotation and modeling work to lower grades and informative genre; PERSUADE likely cited as the baseline argumentative writing resource.

papers.title: Content vs. Form: What Drives the Writing Score Gap Across Socioeconomic Backgrounds?
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — large corpus of persuasive essays by US students used as primary dataset
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE used to decompose SES writing score gaps into content vs. style contributions using LLM-generated essay variants. Novel interdisciplinary use.

papers.title: Development and Validation of an AWE System 'Write On with Cambi!'
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely reference/comparison — PERSUADE likely cited as a key benchmark in the AWE system development background
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. Describes development of an AWE feedback system; PERSUADE likely referenced as a foundational argumentative writing resource.

papers.title: Evaluating Open-Source LLMs for Automated Essay Scoring: The Critical Role of Prompt Design
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — PERSUADE 2.0 used as the primary evaluation dataset across all experiments
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE 2.0 used to benchmark five open-source LLMs across three prompting strategies; rubric-aligned prompting found to be the key driver of human-aligned scoring.

papers.title: Don't Score too Early! Evaluating Argument Mining Models on Incomplete Essays
institutions: Leibniz Institute for Science and Mathematics Education
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — PERSUADE used alongside the German DARIUS corpus as one of two primary evaluation corpora for testing AM robustness on incomplete texts
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE used to evaluate how AM models perform on fragmented vs. complete essays; sentence-level classifiers found more robust than token-level methods under incomplete conditions.

papers.title: Intellectual Technology for Detecting Text Deepfakes Using Large Language Models
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely direct — paper analyzes Kaggle 'LLM - Detect AI Generated Text' competition which used PERSUADE as the human-written essay source
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. PERSUADE used indirectly via the Kaggle competition dataset; notable as a non-English language citation reflecting international reach.

papers.title: The Order Matters: Sequential Fine-tuning of LLaMA for Coherent Automated Essay Scoring
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — PERSUADE 2.0 used as the primary corpus for all experiments
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE 2.0 used to show that sequential fine-tuning aligned with discourse structure outperforms independent and randomized training approaches.

papers.title: Marked Pedagogies: Examining Linguistic Biases in Personalized Automated Writing Feedback
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — PERSUADE used as the primary corpus
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. PERSUADE used to show that LLMs exhibit Marked Pedagogies: biased instructional orientations drawing from assumptions about student learning needs and identities — with White and high-achieving students receiving growth-oriented critique while feedback for Hispanic, ELL, non-White, and female students invoked stereotypes and withheld substantive critique.

papers.title: Accuracy and fairness of generative AI in automated essay scoring: Comparing GPT-4o, feature-based models, and human raters
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — PERSUADE 2.0 used as primary evaluation corpus

papers.title: Spotlights and Blindspots: Evaluating Machine-Generated Text Detection
papers.abstract: We evaluate 15 different detection models from six distinct systems, as well as seven trained models, across seven English-language textual test sets including PERSUADE student essays.
papers.use_of_dataset: Reference/comparison — PERSUADE as one of 7 evaluation test sets

papers.title: A Human-Centered Framework for Exploring and Steering LLM Internals: A Case Study of Bias in Essay Scoring
papers.abstract: Framework reframes LLM interpretability as sensemaking, examining demographic bias in LLM-based essay scoring through activation steering and human-centered analysis.
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — PERSUADE used as the essay corpus for bias analysis

papers.title: Both Quantity and Quality Matter: Investigating the Presence and Effectiveness of Key Argumentative Elements in the Persuasive Writing of U.S. Secondary Students
papers.abstract: Examines argumentative elements in 13,121 essays from PERSUADE 2.0 corpus using Dirichlet modeling to investigate how quantity and quality of key argumentative elements predict essay quality.
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — PERSUADE 2.0 is the primary corpus (13,121 essays)

papers.title: S-GRADES: Studying Generalization of Student Response Assessments in Diverse Evaluative Settings
papers.abstract: Introduces S-GRADES, a unified benchmark consolidating 14 grading datasets spanning Automated Essay Scoring and Automatic Short Answer Grading. Evaluates three LLMs across prompting strategies, examining cross-dataset transfer and generalization gaps.
papers.use_of_dataset: Reference/comparison — PERSUADE is one of 14 grading datasets in the benchmark
